nextToken.description: If the amount of returned information is significantly large, an identifier is also returned. It can be used in a subsequent list action types call to return the next set of action types in the list.

category.description:
A category defines what kind of action can be taken in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the action. Valid categories are limited to one of the following values.

  • Source

  • Build

  • Test

  • Deploy

  • Invoke

  • Approval

  • Compute

owner.description: The creator of the action being called. There are three valid values for the Owner field in the action category section within your pipeline structure: AWS, ThirdParty, and Custom. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

provider.description: The provider of the service being called by the action. Valid providers are determined by the action category. For example, an action in the Deploy category type might have a provider of CodeDeploy, which would be specified as CodeDeploy. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

version.description: A string that describes the action version.

thirdPartyConfigurationUrl.description: The URL of a sign-up page where users can sign up for an external service and perform initial configuration of the action provided by that service.

entityUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that provides a deep link to the resources of the external system, such as the configuration page for a CodeDeploy deployment group. This link is provided as part of the action display in the pipeline.

executionUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that contains a link to the top-level landing page for the external system, such as the console page for CodeDeploy. This link is shown on the pipeline view page in the CodePipeline console and provides a link to the execution entity of the external action.

revisionUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that contains a link to the page where customers can update or change the configuration of the external action.

actionConfigurationProperties.description: The configuration properties for the action type.

inputArtifactDetails.description: The details of the input artifact for the action, such as its commit ID.

minimumCount.description: The minimum number of artifacts allowed for the action type.

maximumCount.description: The maximum number of artifacts allowed for the action type.

outputArtifactDetails.description: The details of the output artifact of the action, such as its commit ID.

minimumCount.description: The minimum number of artifacts allowed for the action type.

maximumCount.description: The maximum number of artifacts allowed for the action type.

nextToken.description: If the amount of returned information is significantly large, an identifier is also returned. It can be used in a subsequent list pipelines call to return the next set of pipelines in the list.

pipelineType.description:
CodePipeline provides the following pipeline types, which differ in characteristics and price, so that you can tailor your pipeline features and cost to the needs of your applications.

  • V1 type pipelines have a JSON structure that contains standard pipeline, stage, and action-level parameters.

  • V2 type pipelines have the same structure as a V1 type, along with additional parameters for release safety and trigger configuration.

Including V2 parameters, such as triggers on Git tags, in the pipeline JSON when creating or updating a pipeline will result in the pipeline having the V2 type of pipeline and the associated costs.

For information about pricing for CodePipeline, see Pricing.

For information about which type of pipeline to choose, see What type of pipeline is right for me?.

executionMode.description: The method that the pipeline will use to handle multiple executions. The default mode is SUPERSEDED.

created.description: The date and time the pipeline was created, in timestamp format.

updated.description: The date and time of the last update to the pipeline, in timestamp format.

ruleTypes.description: Lists the rules that are configured for the condition.

category.description: A category defines what kind of rule can be run in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the rule. The valid category is Rule.

owner.description: The creator of the rule being called. The valid value for the Owner field in the rule category is AWS.

provider.description: The rule provider, such as the DeploymentWindow rule. For a list of rule provider names, see the rules listed in the CodePipeline rule reference.

version.description: A string that describes the rule version.

settings.description: Returns information about the settings for a rule type.

thirdPartyConfigurationUrl.description: The URL of a sign-up page where users can sign up for an external service and perform initial configuration of the action provided by that service.

entityUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that provides a deep link to the resources of the external system, such as the configuration page for a CodeDeploy deployment group. This link is provided as part of the action display in the pipeline.

executionUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that contains a link to the top-level landing page for the external system, such as the console page for CodeDeploy. This link is shown on the pipeline view page in the CodePipeline console and provides a link to the execution entity of the external action.

revisionUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that contains a link to the page where customers can update or change the configuration of the external action.

ruleConfigurationProperties.description: The configuration properties for the rule type.

inputArtifactDetails.description: Returns information about the details of an artifact.

minimumCount.description: The minimum number of artifacts allowed for the action type.

maximumCount.description: The maximum number of artifacts allowed for the action type.

webhooks.description: The JSON detail returned for each webhook in the list output for the ListWebhooks call.

NextToken.description: If the amount of returned information is significantly large, an identifier is also returned and can be used in a subsequent ListWebhooks call to return the next set of webhooks in the list.

definition.description: The detail returned for each webhook, such as the webhook authentication type and filter rules.

targetPipeline.description: The name of the pipeline you want to connect to the webhook.

targetAction.description: The name of the action in a pipeline you want to connect to the webhook. The action must be from the source (first) stage of the pipeline.

filters.description: A list of rules applied to the body/payload sent in the POST request to a webhook URL. All defined rules must pass for the request to be accepted and the pipeline started.

authentication.description:
Supported options are GITHUB_HMAC, IP, and UNAUTHENTICATED.

When creating CodePipeline webhooks, do not use your own credentials or reuse the same secret token across multiple webhooks. For optimal security, generate a unique secret token for each webhook you create. The secret token is an arbitrary string that you provide, which GitHub uses to compute and sign the webhook payloads sent to CodePipeline, for protecting the integrity and authenticity of the webhook payloads. Using your own credentials or reusing the same token across multiple webhooks can lead to security vulnerabilities.

If a secret token was provided, it will be redacted in the response.

  • For information about the authentication scheme implemented by GITHUB_HMAC, see Securing your webhooks on the GitHub Developer website.

  • IP rejects webhooks trigger requests unless they originate from an IP address in the IP range whitelisted in the authentication configuration.

  • UNAUTHENTICATED accepts all webhook trigger requests regardless of origin.

authenticationConfiguration.description: Properties that configure the authentication applied to incoming webhook trigger requests. The required properties depend on the authentication type. For GITHUB_HMAC, only the SecretToken property must be set. For IP, only the AllowedIPRange property must be set to a valid CIDR range. For UNAUTHENTICATED, no properties can be set.

AllowedIPRange.description: The property used to configure acceptance of webhooks in an IP address range. For IP, only the AllowedIPRange property must be set. This property must be set to a valid CIDR range.

SecretToken.description:
The property used to configure GitHub authentication. For GITHUB_HMAC, only the SecretToken property must be set.

When creating CodePipeline webhooks, do not use your own credentials or reuse the same secret token across multiple webhooks. For optimal security, generate a unique secret token for each webhook you create. The secret token is an arbitrary string that you provide, which GitHub uses to compute and sign the webhook payloads sent to CodePipeline, for protecting the integrity and authenticity of the webhook payloads. Using your own credentials or reusing the same token across multiple webhooks can lead to security vulnerabilities.

If a secret token was provided, it will be redacted in the response.

url.description: A unique URL generated by CodePipeline. When a POST request is made to this URL, the defined pipeline is started as long as the body of the post request satisfies the defined authentication and filtering conditions. Deleting and re-creating a webhook makes the old URL invalid and generates a new one.

errorMessage.description: The text of the error message about the webhook.

lastTriggered.description: The date and time a webhook was last successfully triggered, in timestamp format.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the webhook.

tags.description: Specifies the tags applied to the webhook.

status.description: Whether the job worker has received the specified job.
The status information for the third party job, if any.

actionType.description: Returns information about the details of an action type.

tags.description: Specifies the tags applied to the custom action.

pipeline.description: Represents the structure of actions and stages to be performed in the pipeline.

tags.description: Specifies the tags applied to the pipeline.

actionType.description: The action type information for the requested action type, such as the action type ID.

description.description: The description for the action type to be updated.

executor.description: Information about the executor for an action type that was created with any supported integration model.

configuration.description: The action configuration properties for the action type. These properties are specified in the action definition when the action type is created.

lambdaExecutorConfiguration.description: Details about the Lambda executor of the action type.

lambdaFunctionArn.description: The ARN of the Lambda function used by the action engine.

jobWorkerExecutorConfiguration.description: Details about the JobWorker executor of the action type.

pollingAccounts.description: The accounts in which the job worker is configured and might poll for jobs as part of the action execution.

pollingServicePrincipals.description: The service Principals in which the job worker is configured and might poll for jobs as part of the action execution.

type.description: The integration model used to create and update the action type, Lambda or JobWorker.

policyStatementsTemplate.description:
The policy statement that specifies the permissions in the CodePipeline customer account that are needed to successfully run an action.

To grant permission to another account, specify the account ID as the Principal, a domain-style identifier defined by the service, for example codepipeline.amazonaws.com.

The size of the passed JSON policy document cannot exceed 2048 characters.

jobTimeout.description: The timeout in seconds for the job. An action execution can have multiple jobs. This is the timeout for a single job, not the entire action execution.

id.description: The action category, owner, provider, and version of the action type to be updated.

category.description:
Defines what kind of action can be taken in the stage, one of the following:

  • Source

  • Build

  • Test

  • Deploy

  • Approval

  • Invoke

owner.description: The creator of the action type being called: AWS or ThirdParty.

provider.description: The provider of the action type being called. The provider name is supplied when the action type is created.

version.description: A string that describes the action type version.

inputArtifactDetails.description: Details for the artifacts, such as application files, to be worked on by the action. For example, the minimum and maximum number of input artifacts allowed.

minimumCount.description: The minimum number of artifacts that can be used with the action type. For example, you should specify a minimum and maximum of zero input artifacts for an action type with a category of source.

maximumCount.description: The maximum number of artifacts that can be used with the actiontype. For example, you should specify a minimum and maximum of zero input artifacts for an action type with a category of source.

outputArtifactDetails.description: Details for the output artifacts, such as a built application, that are the result of the action. For example, the minimum and maximum number of output artifacts allowed.

minimumCount.description: The minimum number of artifacts that can be used with the action type. For example, you should specify a minimum and maximum of zero input artifacts for an action type with a category of source.

maximumCount.description: The maximum number of artifacts that can be used with the actiontype. For example, you should specify a minimum and maximum of zero input artifacts for an action type with a category of source.

permissions.description: Details identifying the accounts with permissions to use the action type.

allowedAccounts.description: A list of Amazon Web Services account IDs with access to use the action type in their pipelines.

properties.description: The properties of the action type to be updated.

urls.description: The links associated with the action type to be updated.

configurationUrl.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that contains a link to the page where customers can configure the external action.

entityUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that provides a deep link to the resources of the external system, such as a status page. This link is provided as part of the action display in the pipeline.

executionUrlTemplate.description: The link to an execution page for the action type in progress. For example, for a CodeDeploy action, this link is shown on the pipeline view page in the CodePipeline console, and it links to a CodeDeploy status page.

revisionUrlTemplate.description: The URL returned to the CodePipeline console that contains a link to the page where customers can update or change the configuration of the external action.

jobDetails.description:
The details of the job.

If AWSSessionCredentials is used, a long-running job can call GetJobDetails again to obtain new credentials.

id.description: The unique system-generated ID of the job.

data.description: Represents other information about a job required for a job worker to complete the job.

category.description:
A category defines what kind of action can be taken in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the action. Valid categories are limited to one of the following values.

  • Source

  • Build

  • Test

  • Deploy

  • Invoke

  • Approval

  • Compute

owner.description: The creator of the action being called. There are three valid values for the Owner field in the action category section within your pipeline structure: AWS, ThirdParty, and Custom. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

provider.description: The provider of the service being called by the action. Valid providers are determined by the action category. For example, an action in the Deploy category type might have a provider of CodeDeploy, which would be specified as CodeDeploy. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

version.description: A string that describes the action version.

pipelineContext.description:
Represents information about a pipeline to a job worker.

Includes pipelineArn and pipelineExecutionId for custom jobs.

pipelineName.description: The name of the pipeline. This is a user-specified value. Pipeline names must be unique across all pipeline names under an Amazon Web Services account.

action.description: The context of an action to a job worker in the stage of a pipeline.

name.description: The name of the action in the context of a job.

actionExecutionId.description: The system-generated unique ID that corresponds to an action's execution.

pipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline.

artifactCredentials.description: Represents an Amazon Web Services session credentials object. These credentials are temporary credentials that are issued by Amazon Web Services Secure Token Service (STS). They can be used to access input and output artifacts in the S3 bucket used to store artifacts for the pipeline in CodePipeline.

secretAccessKey.description: The secret access key for the session.

continuationToken.description: A system-generated token, such as a deployment ID, required by a job to continue the job asynchronously.

encryptionKey.description: Represents information about the key used to encrypt data in the artifact store, such as an KMS key.

id.description:
The ID used to identify the key. For an Amazon Web Services KMS key, you can use the key ID, the key ARN, or the alias ARN.

Aliases are recognized only in the account that created the KMS key. For cross-account actions, you can only use the key ID or key ARN to identify the key. Cross-account actions involve using the role from the other account (AccountB), so specifying the key ID will use the key from the other account (AccountB).

type.description: The type of encryption key, such as an Amazon Web Services KMS key. When creating or updating a pipeline, the value must be set to 'KMS'.

accountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the job.

pipeline.description: Represents the structure of actions and stages to be performed in the pipeline.

metadata.description: Represents the pipeline metadata information returned as part of the output of a GetPipeline action.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for CodePipeline to use to either perform actions with no actionRoleArn, or to use to assume roles for actions with an actionRoleArn.

artifactStore.description:
Represents information about the S3 bucket where artifacts are stored for the pipeline.

You must include either artifactStore or artifactStores in your pipeline, but you cannot use both. If you create a cross-region action in your pipeline, you must use artifactStores.

type.description: The type of the artifact store, such as S3.

location.description: The S3 bucket used for storing the artifacts for a pipeline. You can specify the name of an S3 bucket but not a folder in the bucket. A folder to contain the pipeline artifacts is created for you based on the name of the pipeline. You can use any S3 bucket in the same Amazon Web Services Region as the pipeline to store your pipeline artifacts.

encryptionKey.description: The encryption key used to encrypt the data in the artifact store, such as an Amazon Web Services Key Management Service key. If this is undefined, the default key for Amazon S3 is used.

id.description:
The ID used to identify the key. For an Amazon Web Services KMS key, you can use the key ID, the key ARN, or the alias ARN.

Aliases are recognized only in the account that created the KMS key. For cross-account actions, you can only use the key ID or key ARN to identify the key. Cross-account actions involve using the role from the other account (AccountB), so specifying the key ID will use the key from the other account (AccountB).

type.description: The type of encryption key, such as an Amazon Web Services KMS key. When creating or updating a pipeline, the value must be set to 'KMS'.

artifactStores.description:
A mapping of artifactStore objects and their corresponding Amazon Web Services Regions. There must be an artifact store for the pipeline Region and for each cross-region action in the pipeline.

You must include either artifactStore or artifactStores in your pipeline, but you cannot use both. If you create a cross-region action in your pipeline, you must use artifactStores.

stages.description: The stage in which to perform the action.

version.description: The version number of the pipeline. A new pipeline always has a version number of 1. This number is incremented when a pipeline is updated.

executionMode.description: The method that the pipeline will use to handle multiple executions. The default mode is SUPERSEDED.

pipelineType.description:
CodePipeline provides the following pipeline types, which differ in characteristics and price, so that you can tailor your pipeline features and cost to the needs of your applications.

  • V1 type pipelines have a JSON structure that contains standard pipeline, stage, and action-level parameters.

  • V2 type pipelines have the same structure as a V1 type, along with additional parameters for release safety and trigger configuration.

Including V2 parameters, such as triggers on Git tags, in the pipeline JSON when creating or updating a pipeline will result in the pipeline having the V2 type of pipeline and the associated costs.

For information about pricing for CodePipeline, see Pricing.

For information about which type of pipeline to choose, see What type of pipeline is right for me?.

variables.description: A list that defines the pipeline variables for a pipeline resource. Variable names can have alphanumeric and underscore characters, and the values must match [A-Za-z0-9@\-_]+.

triggers.description:
The trigger configuration specifying a type of event, such as Git tags, that starts the pipeline.

When a trigger configuration is specified, default change detection for repository and branch commits is disabled.

pipelineExecution.description: Represents information about the execution of a pipeline.

pipelineName.description: The name of the pipeline with the specified pipeline execution.

pipelineVersion.description: The version number of the pipeline with the specified pipeline execution.

status.description:
The status of the pipeline execution.

  • Cancelled: The pipeline’s definition was updated before the pipeline execution could be completed.

  • InProgress: The pipeline execution is currently running.

  • Stopped: The pipeline execution was manually stopped. For more information, see Stopped Executions.

  • Stopping: The pipeline execution received a request to be manually stopped. Depending on the selected stop mode, the execution is either completing or abandoning in-progress actions. For more information, see Stopped Executions.

  • Succeeded: The pipeline execution was completed successfully.

  • Superseded: While this pipeline execution was waiting for the next stage to be completed, a newer pipeline execution advanced and continued through the pipeline instead. For more information, see Superseded Executions.

  • Failed: The pipeline execution was not completed successfully.

statusSummary.description: A summary that contains a description of the pipeline execution status.

artifactRevisions.description: A list of ArtifactRevision objects included in a pipeline execution.

variables.description: A list of pipeline variables used for the pipeline execution.

trigger.description: The interaction or event that started a pipeline execution.

triggerType.description: The type of change-detection method, command, or user interaction that started a pipeline execution.

triggerDetail.description: Detail related to the event that started a pipeline execution, such as the webhook ARN of the webhook that triggered the pipeline execution or the user ARN for a user-initiated start-pipeline-execution CLI command.

executionMode.description: The method that the pipeline will use to handle multiple executions. The default mode is SUPERSEDED.

rollbackMetadata.description: The metadata about the execution pertaining to stage rollback.

rollbackTargetPipelineExecutionId.description: The pipeline execution ID to which the stage will be rolled back.

pipelineName.description: The name of the pipeline for which you want to get the state.

pipelineVersion.description:
The version number of the pipeline.

A newly created pipeline is always assigned a version number of 1.

stageStates.description: A list of the pipeline stage output information, including stage name, state, most recent run details, whether the stage is disabled, and other data.

created.description: The date and time the pipeline was created, in timestamp format.

updated.description: The date and time the pipeline was last updated, in timestamp format.

inboundExecution.description: Represents information about the run of a stage.

pipelineExecutionId.description: The ID of the pipeline execution associated with the stage.

status.description:
The status of the stage, or for a completed stage, the last status of the stage.

A status of cancelled means that the pipeline’s definition was updated before the stage execution could be completed.

type.description: The type of pipeline execution for the stage, such as a rollback pipeline execution.

inboundTransitionState.description: The state of the inbound transition, which is either enabled or disabled.

enabled.description: Whether the transition between stages is enabled (true) or disabled (false).

lastChangedBy.description: The ID of the user who last changed the transition state.

lastChangedAt.description: The timestamp when the transition state was last changed.

disabledReason.description: The user-specified reason why the transition between two stages of a pipeline was disabled.

latestExecution.description: Information about the latest execution in the stage, including its ID and status.

pipelineExecutionId.description: The ID of the pipeline execution associated with the stage.

status.description:
The status of the stage, or for a completed stage, the last status of the stage.

A status of cancelled means that the pipeline’s definition was updated before the stage execution could be completed.

type.description: The type of pipeline execution for the stage, such as a rollback pipeline execution.

beforeEntryConditionState.description: The state of the entry conditions for a stage.

latestExecution.description: Represents information about the latest run of a condition for a stage.

status.description: The status of a run of a condition for a stage.

summary.description: A summary of the run of the condition for a stage.

conditionStates.description: The states of the conditions for a run of a condition for a stage.

onSuccessConditionState.description: The state of the success conditions for a stage.

latestExecution.description: Represents information about the latest run of a condition for a stage.

status.description: The status of a run of a condition for a stage.

summary.description: A summary of the run of the condition for a stage.

conditionStates.description: The states of the conditions for a run of a condition for a stage.

onFailureConditionState.description: The state of the failure conditions for a stage.

latestExecution.description: Represents information about the latest run of a condition for a stage.

status.description: The status of a run of a condition for a stage.

summary.description: A summary of the run of the condition for a stage.

conditionStates.description: The states of the conditions for a run of a condition for a stage.

retryStageMetadata.description: he details of a specific automatic retry on stage failure, including the attempt number and trigger.

autoStageRetryAttempt.description: The number of attempts for a specific stage with automatic retry on stage failure. One attempt is allowed for automatic stage retry on failure.

manualStageRetryAttempt.description: The number of attempts for a specific stage where manual retries have been made upon stage failure.

latestRetryTrigger.description: The latest trigger for a specific stage where manual or automatic retries have been made upon stage failure.

jobDetails.description: The details of the job, including any protected values defined for the job.

id.description: The identifier used to identify the job details in CodePipeline.

data.description: The data to be returned by the third party job worker.

category.description:
A category defines what kind of action can be taken in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the action. Valid categories are limited to one of the following values.

  • Source

  • Build

  • Test

  • Deploy

  • Invoke

  • Approval

  • Compute

owner.description: The creator of the action being called. There are three valid values for the Owner field in the action category section within your pipeline structure: AWS, ThirdParty, and Custom. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

provider.description: The provider of the service being called by the action. Valid providers are determined by the action category. For example, an action in the Deploy category type might have a provider of CodeDeploy, which would be specified as CodeDeploy. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

version.description: A string that describes the action version.

pipelineContext.description:
Represents information about a pipeline to a job worker.

Does not include pipelineArn and pipelineExecutionId for ThirdParty jobs.

pipelineName.description: The name of the pipeline. This is a user-specified value. Pipeline names must be unique across all pipeline names under an Amazon Web Services account.

action.description: The context of an action to a job worker in the stage of a pipeline.

name.description: The name of the action in the context of a job.

actionExecutionId.description: The system-generated unique ID that corresponds to an action's execution.

pipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline.

inputArtifacts.description: The name of the artifact that is worked on by the action, if any. This name might be system-generated, such as "MyApp", or it might be defined by the user when the action is created. The input artifact name must match the name of an output artifact generated by an action in an earlier action or stage of the pipeline.

outputArtifacts.description: The name of the artifact that is the result of the action, if any. This name might be system-generated, such as "MyBuiltApp", or it might be defined by the user when the action is created.

artifactCredentials.description: Represents an Amazon Web Services session credentials object. These credentials are temporary credentials that are issued by Amazon Web Services Secure Token Service (STS). They can be used to access input and output artifacts in the S3 bucket used to store artifact for the pipeline in CodePipeline.

secretAccessKey.description: The secret access key for the session.

continuationToken.description: A system-generated token, such as a CodeDeploy deployment ID, that a job requires to continue the job asynchronously.

encryptionKey.description: The encryption key used to encrypt and decrypt data in the artifact store for the pipeline, such as an Amazon Web Services Key Management Service (Amazon Web Services KMS) key. This is optional and might not be present.

id.description:
The ID used to identify the key. For an Amazon Web Services KMS key, you can use the key ID, the key ARN, or the alias ARN.

Aliases are recognized only in the account that created the KMS key. For cross-account actions, you can only use the key ID or key ARN to identify the key. Cross-account actions involve using the role from the other account (AccountB), so specifying the key ID will use the key from the other account (AccountB).

type.description: The type of encryption key, such as an Amazon Web Services KMS key. When creating or updating a pipeline, the value must be set to 'KMS'.

nonce.description: A system-generated random number that CodePipeline uses to ensure that the job is being worked on by only one job worker. Use this number in an AcknowledgeThirdPartyJob request.

actionExecutionDetails.description: The details for a list of recent executions, such as action execution ID.

nextToken.description: If the amount of returned information is significantly large, an identifier is also returned and can be used in a subsequent ListActionExecutions call to return the next set of action executions in the list.

pipelineExecutionId.description: The pipeline execution ID for the action execution.

pipelineVersion.description: The version of the pipeline where the action was run.

stageName.description: The name of the stage that contains the action.

startTime.description: The start time of the action execution.

lastUpdateTime.description: The last update time of the action execution.

updatedBy.description: The ARN of the user who changed the pipeline execution details.

status.description: The status of the action execution. Status categories are InProgress, Succeeded, and Failed.

input.description: Input details for the action execution, such as role ARN, Region, and input artifacts.

category.description:
A category defines what kind of action can be taken in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the action. Valid categories are limited to one of the following values.

  • Source

  • Build

  • Test

  • Deploy

  • Invoke

  • Approval

  • Compute

owner.description: The creator of the action being called. There are three valid values for the Owner field in the action category section within your pipeline structure: AWS, ThirdParty, and Custom. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

provider.description: The provider of the service being called by the action. Valid providers are determined by the action category. For example, an action in the Deploy category type might have a provider of CodeDeploy, which would be specified as CodeDeploy. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

version.description: A string that describes the action version.

resolvedConfiguration.description: Configuration data for an action execution with all variable references replaced with their real values for the execution.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM service role that performs the declared action. This is assumed through the roleArn for the pipeline.

region.description: The Amazon Web Services Region for the action, such as us-east-1.

inputArtifacts.description: Details of input artifacts of the action that correspond to the action execution.

namespace.description: The variable namespace associated with the action. All variables produced as output by this action fall under this namespace.

output.description: Output details for the action execution, such as the action execution result.

outputArtifacts.description: Details of output artifacts of the action that correspond to the action execution.

executionResult.description: Execution result information listed in the output details for an action execution.

externalExecutionId.description: The action provider's external ID for the action execution.

externalExecutionSummary.description: The action provider's summary for the action execution.

externalExecutionUrl.description: The deepest external link to the external resource (for example, a repository URL or deployment endpoint) that is used when running the action.

errorDetails.description: Represents information about an error in CodePipeline.

code.description: The system ID or number code of the error.

logStreamARN.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the log stream for the action compute.

outputVariables.description: The outputVariables field shows the key-value pairs that were output as part of that execution.

nextToken.description: An identifier that was returned from the previous list action types call, which can be used to return the next set of action types in the list.

targetId.description: The ID of the target for the deploy action.

targetType.description: The type of target for the deploy action.

startTime.description: The start time for the deploy action.

endTime.description: The end time for the deploy action.

events.description: The lifecycle events for the deploy action.

pipelineExecutionSummaries.description: A list of executions in the history of a pipeline.

nextToken.description: A token that can be used in the next ListPipelineExecutions call. To view all items in the list, continue to call this operation with each subsequent token until no more nextToken values are returned.

status.description:
The status of the pipeline execution.

  • InProgress: The pipeline execution is currently running.

  • Stopped: The pipeline execution was manually stopped. For more information, see Stopped Executions.

  • Stopping: The pipeline execution received a request to be manually stopped. Depending on the selected stop mode, the execution is either completing or abandoning in-progress actions. For more information, see Stopped Executions.

  • Succeeded: The pipeline execution was completed successfully.

  • Superseded: While this pipeline execution was waiting for the next stage to be completed, a newer pipeline execution advanced and continued through the pipeline instead. For more information, see Superseded Executions.

  • Failed: The pipeline execution was not completed successfully.

startTime.description: The date and time when the pipeline execution began, in timestamp format.

lastUpdateTime.description: The date and time of the last change to the pipeline execution, in timestamp format.

sourceRevisions.description: A list of the source artifact revisions that initiated a pipeline execution.

trigger.description: The interaction or event that started a pipeline execution, such as automated change detection or a StartPipelineExecution API call.

triggerType.description: The type of change-detection method, command, or user interaction that started a pipeline execution.

triggerDetail.description: Detail related to the event that started a pipeline execution, such as the webhook ARN of the webhook that triggered the pipeline execution or the user ARN for a user-initiated start-pipeline-execution CLI command.

stopTrigger.description: The interaction that stopped a pipeline execution.

reason.description: The user-specified reason the pipeline was stopped.

executionMode.description: The method that the pipeline will use to handle multiple executions. The default mode is SUPERSEDED.

rollbackMetadata.description: The metadata for the stage execution to be rolled back.

rollbackTargetPipelineExecutionId.description: The pipeline execution ID to which the stage will be rolled back.

ruleExecutionDetails.description: Details about the output for listing rule executions.

nextToken.description: A token that can be used in the next ListRuleExecutions call. To view all items in the list, continue to call this operation with each subsequent token until no more nextToken values are returned.

pipelineExecutionId.description: The ID of the pipeline execution in the stage where the rule was run. Use the GetPipelineState action to retrieve the current pipelineExecutionId of the stage.

ruleExecutionId.description: The ID of the run for the rule.

pipelineVersion.description: The version number of the pipeline with the stage where the rule was run.

stageName.description: The name of the stage where the rule was run.

ruleName.description: The name of the rule that was run in the stage.

startTime.description: The start time of the rule execution.

lastUpdateTime.description: The date and time of the last change to the rule execution, in timestamp format.

updatedBy.description: The ARN of the user who changed the rule execution details.

status.description: The status of the rule execution. Status categories are InProgress, Succeeded, and Failed.

input.description: Input details for the rule execution, such as role ARN, Region, and input artifacts.

ruleTypeId.description: The ID for the rule type, which is made up of the combined values for category, owner, provider, and version. For more information about conditions, see Stage conditions. For more information about rules, see the CodePipeline rule reference.

category.description: A category defines what kind of rule can be run in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the rule. The valid category is Rule.

owner.description: The creator of the rule being called. The valid value for the Owner field in the rule category is AWS.

provider.description: The rule provider, such as the DeploymentWindow rule. For a list of rule provider names, see the rules listed in the CodePipeline rule reference.

version.description: A string that describes the rule version.

configuration.description: Configuration data for a rule execution, such as the resolved values for that run.

resolvedConfiguration.description: Configuration data for a rule execution with all variable references replaced with their real values for the execution.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM service role that performs the declared rule. This is assumed through the roleArn for the pipeline.

region.description: The Amazon Web Services Region for the rule, such as us-east-1.

inputArtifacts.description: Details of input artifacts of the rule that correspond to the rule execution.

output.description: Output details for the rule execution, such as the rule execution result.

executionResult.description: Execution result information listed in the output details for a rule execution.

externalExecutionId.description: The external ID for the rule execution.

externalExecutionSummary.description: The external provider summary for the rule execution.

externalExecutionUrl.description: The deepest external link to the external resource (for example, a repository URL or deployment endpoint) that is used when running the rule.

errorDetails.description: Represents information about an error in CodePipeline.

code.description: The system ID or number code of the error.

nextToken.description: If the amount of returned information is significantly large, an identifier is also returned and can be used in a subsequent API call to return the next page of the list. The ListTagsforResource call lists all available tags in one call and does not use pagination.

jobs.description: Information about the jobs to take action on.

id.description: The unique system-generated ID of the job.

category.description:
A category defines what kind of action can be taken in the stage, and constrains the provider type for the action. Valid categories are limited to one of the following values.

  • Source

  • Build

  • Test

  • Deploy

  • Invoke

  • Approval

  • Compute

owner.description: The creator of the action being called. There are three valid values for the Owner field in the action category section within your pipeline structure: AWS, ThirdParty, and Custom. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

provider.description: The provider of the service being called by the action. Valid providers are determined by the action category. For example, an action in the Deploy category type might have a provider of CodeDeploy, which would be specified as CodeDeploy. For more information, see Valid Action Types and Providers in CodePipeline.

version.description: A string that describes the action version.

pipelineContext.description:
Represents information about a pipeline to a job worker.

Includes pipelineArn and pipelineExecutionId for custom jobs.

pipelineName.description: The name of the pipeline. This is a user-specified value. Pipeline names must be unique across all pipeline names under an Amazon Web Services account.

action.description: The context of an action to a job worker in the stage of a pipeline.

name.description: The name of the action in the context of a job.

actionExecutionId.description: The system-generated unique ID that corresponds to an action's execution.

pipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline.

artifactCredentials.description: Represents an Amazon Web Services session credentials object. These credentials are temporary credentials that are issued by Amazon Web Services Secure Token Service (STS). They can be used to access input and output artifacts in the S3 bucket used to store artifacts for the pipeline in CodePipeline.

secretAccessKey.description: The secret access key for the session.

continuationToken.description: A system-generated token, such as a deployment ID, required by a job to continue the job asynchronously.

encryptionKey.description: Represents information about the key used to encrypt data in the artifact store, such as an KMS key.

id.description:
The ID used to identify the key. For an Amazon Web Services KMS key, you can use the key ID, the key ARN, or the alias ARN.

Aliases are recognized only in the account that created the KMS key. For cross-account actions, you can only use the key ID or key ARN to identify the key. Cross-account actions involve using the role from the other account (AccountB), so specifying the key ID will use the key from the other account (AccountB).

type.description: The type of encryption key, such as an Amazon Web Services KMS key. When creating or updating a pipeline, the value must be set to 'KMS'.

nonce.description: A system-generated random number that CodePipeline uses to ensure that the job is being worked on by only one job worker. Use this number in an AcknowledgeJob request.

accountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account to use when performing the job.

jobs.description: Information about the jobs to take action on.

clientId.description: The clientToken portion of the clientId and clientToken pair used to verify that the calling entity is allowed access to the job and its details.

jobId.description: The identifier used to identify the job in CodePipeline.

newRevision.description: Indicates whether the artifact revision was previously used in an execution of the specified pipeline.

pipelineExecutionId.description: The ID of the current workflow state of the pipeline.

approvedAt.description: The timestamp showing when the approval or rejection was submitted.

webhook.description: The detail returned from creating the webhook, such as the webhook name, webhook URL, and webhook ARN.

definition.description: The detail returned for each webhook, such as the webhook authentication type and filter rules.

targetPipeline.description: The name of the pipeline you want to connect to the webhook.

targetAction.description: The name of the action in a pipeline you want to connect to the webhook. The action must be from the source (first) stage of the pipeline.

filters.description: A list of rules applied to the body/payload sent in the POST request to a webhook URL. All defined rules must pass for the request to be accepted and the pipeline started.

authentication.description:
Supported options are GITHUB_HMAC, IP, and UNAUTHENTICATED.

When creating CodePipeline webhooks, do not use your own credentials or reuse the same secret token across multiple webhooks. For optimal security, generate a unique secret token for each webhook you create. The secret token is an arbitrary string that you provide, which GitHub uses to compute and sign the webhook payloads sent to CodePipeline, for protecting the integrity and authenticity of the webhook payloads. Using your own credentials or reusing the same token across multiple webhooks can lead to security vulnerabilities.

If a secret token was provided, it will be redacted in the response.

  • For information about the authentication scheme implemented by GITHUB_HMAC, see Securing your webhooks on the GitHub Developer website.

  • IP rejects webhooks trigger requests unless they originate from an IP address in the IP range whitelisted in the authentication configuration.

  • UNAUTHENTICATED accepts all webhook trigger requests regardless of origin.

authenticationConfiguration.description: Properties that configure the authentication applied to incoming webhook trigger requests. The required properties depend on the authentication type. For GITHUB_HMAC, only the SecretToken property must be set. For IP, only the AllowedIPRange property must be set to a valid CIDR range. For UNAUTHENTICATED, no properties can be set.

AllowedIPRange.description: The property used to configure acceptance of webhooks in an IP address range. For IP, only the AllowedIPRange property must be set. This property must be set to a valid CIDR range.

SecretToken.description:
The property used to configure GitHub authentication. For GITHUB_HMAC, only the SecretToken property must be set.

When creating CodePipeline webhooks, do not use your own credentials or reuse the same secret token across multiple webhooks. For optimal security, generate a unique secret token for each webhook you create. The secret token is an arbitrary string that you provide, which GitHub uses to compute and sign the webhook payloads sent to CodePipeline, for protecting the integrity and authenticity of the webhook payloads. Using your own credentials or reusing the same token across multiple webhooks can lead to security vulnerabilities.

If a secret token was provided, it will be redacted in the response.

url.description: A unique URL generated by CodePipeline. When a POST request is made to this URL, the defined pipeline is started as long as the body of the post request satisfies the defined authentication and filtering conditions. Deleting and re-creating a webhook makes the old URL invalid and generates a new one.

errorMessage.description: The text of the error message about the webhook.

lastTriggered.description: The date and time a webhook was last successfully triggered, in timestamp format.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the webhook.

tags.description: Specifies the tags applied to the webhook.

pipelineExecutionId.description: The ID of the current workflow execution in the failed stage.
The execution ID of the pipeline execution for the stage that has been rolled back.
The unique system-generated ID of the pipeline execution that was started.
The unique system-generated ID of the pipeline execution that was stopped.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for CodePipeline to use to either perform actions with no actionRoleArn, or to use to assume roles for actions with an actionRoleArn.

artifactStore.description:
Represents information about the S3 bucket where artifacts are stored for the pipeline.

You must include either artifactStore or artifactStores in your pipeline, but you cannot use both. If you create a cross-region action in your pipeline, you must use artifactStores.

type.description: The type of the artifact store, such as S3.

location.description: The S3 bucket used for storing the artifacts for a pipeline. You can specify the name of an S3 bucket but not a folder in the bucket. A folder to contain the pipeline artifacts is created for you based on the name of the pipeline. You can use any S3 bucket in the same Amazon Web Services Region as the pipeline to store your pipeline artifacts.

encryptionKey.description: The encryption key used to encrypt the data in the artifact store, such as an Amazon Web Services Key Management Service key. If this is undefined, the default key for Amazon S3 is used.

id.description:
The ID used to identify the key. For an Amazon Web Services KMS key, you can use the key ID, the key ARN, or the alias ARN.

Aliases are recognized only in the account that created the KMS key. For cross-account actions, you can only use the key ID or key ARN to identify the key. Cross-account actions involve using the role from the other account (AccountB), so specifying the key ID will use the key from the other account (AccountB).

type.description: The type of encryption key, such as an Amazon Web Services KMS key. When creating or updating a pipeline, the value must be set to 'KMS'.

artifactStores.description:
A mapping of artifactStore objects and their corresponding Amazon Web Services Regions. There must be an artifact store for the pipeline Region and for each cross-region action in the pipeline.

You must include either artifactStore or artifactStores in your pipeline, but you cannot use both. If you create a cross-region action in your pipeline, you must use artifactStores.

stages.description: The stage in which to perform the action.

version.description: The version number of the pipeline. A new pipeline always has a version number of 1. This number is incremented when a pipeline is updated.

executionMode.description: The method that the pipeline will use to handle multiple executions. The default mode is SUPERSEDED.

pipelineType.description:
CodePipeline provides the following pipeline types, which differ in characteristics and price, so that you can tailor your pipeline features and cost to the needs of your applications.

  • V1 type pipelines have a JSON structure that contains standard pipeline, stage, and action-level parameters.

  • V2 type pipelines have the same structure as a V1 type, along with additional parameters for release safety and trigger configuration.

Including V2 parameters, such as triggers on Git tags, in the pipeline JSON when creating or updating a pipeline will result in the pipeline having the V2 type of pipeline and the associated costs.

For information about pricing for CodePipeline, see Pricing.

For information about which type of pipeline to choose, see What type of pipeline is right for me?.

variables.description: A list that defines the pipeline variables for a pipeline resource. Variable names can have alphanumeric and underscore characters, and the values must match [A-Za-z0-9@\-_]+.

triggers.description:
The trigger configuration specifying a type of event, such as Git tags, that starts the pipeline.

When a trigger configuration is specified, default change detection for repository and branch commits is disabled.
